title: AuthorizationChain
description: An Agent Authorization Chain (AAC) — a verifiable record of how a human principal's intent flows through one or more agents to produce an action. Every link in the chain is traceable and cryptographically bound. Public domain — Human Root of Trust framework.

id.description: Unique identifier for this authorization chain.

principalId.description: The Human Root of Trust who initiated this chain.

terminalAction.description: The final real-world action this chain authorizes.

links.description: Ordered chain of authorization events from principal to final action. First link is always the human principal.

order.description: Position in chain (0 = human principal, 1+ = agents).

actorId.description: Who/what is authorizing at this link.

authorization.description: Human-readable description of what is being authorized.
examples: Authorize weekly report delivery every Sunday at 08:00

scope.description: Explicit list of action types this link authorizes.

receiptId.description: If this link produced an action, the receipt ID that proves it.

status.description: Current status of this authorization chain.

policyId.description: ID of the policy that governs this chain, if any.

$comment: Public domain. Human Root of Trust framework — humanrootoftrust.org. No rights reserved.
